ENTRY            14306   20250815                             152414306000    1 
SUBENT        14306001   20250815                             152414306001    1 
BIB                 11         39                                 14306001    2 
TITLE      Spin measurements for 147Sm + n resonances: further    14306001    3 
           evidence for nonstatistical effects                    14306001    4 
AUTHOR     (P.E.Koehler,J.L.Ullmann,T.A.Bredeweg,J.M.O'Donnell,   14306001    5 
           R.Reifarth,R.S.Rundberg,D.J.Vieira,J.M.Wouters,        14306001    6 
           F.Becvar, M.Krticka,G.E.Mitchell)                      14306001    7 
INSTITUTE  (1USAORL,1USALAS,3CZRCHU,1USATNL,1USANCS)              14306001    8 
REFERENCE  (J,PR/C,76,025804,2007)                                14306001    9 
           #doi:10.1103/PhysRevC.76.025804                        14306001   10 
           (J,NIM/A,647,73,2011) Subsequent re-analysis.          14306001   11 
           #doi:10.1016/j.nima.2011.04.026                        14306001   12 
FACILITY   (LINAC,1USALAS) Experiment was performed using DANCE,  14306001   13 
           located on flight path 14 at the Manuel Lujan, Jr.     14306001   14 
           Neutron scattering center at LANSCE.                   14306001   15 
SAMPLE     (62-SM-147,ENR=0.9793) Three samples of metallic       14306001   16 
           samarium, enriched to 97.93% in 147 Sm, 1 cm in        14306001   17 
           diameter, and weighed 1.444, 3.208, and 10.410 mg      14306001   18 
           were used during the measurements.                     14306001   19 
DETECTOR   (BAF2) DANCE is a 4pi array of 160 BaF2 crystals       14306001   20 
           designed for studying neutron capture cross sections   14306001   21 
           on small samples. DANCE was located on flight path 14, 14306001   22 
           positioned 20 m from the ambient-temperature water     14306001   23 
           moderator.                                             14306001   24 
METHOD     (TOF) Incident neutron energy was determined by time   14306001   25 
           of flight. Width of the proton pulses was 125 ns and   14306001   26 
           flight path was 20 m.                                  14306001   27 
INC-SOURCE (SPALL) Neutrons are generated at LANSCE via           14306001   28 
           spallation reactions when an 800 MeV proton beam       14306001   29 
           strikes a tungsten target. The resulting neutron flux  14306001   30 
           peaks near thermal energy and is approximately         14306001   31 
           proportional to 1/En over the range of measurements.   14306001   32 
HISTORY    (20110928C) Compiled by S.H.                           14306001   33 
           (20201102A) BP: Added subsequent analysis reference    14306001   34 
           and authors, updated data on 147Sm, added doi and      14306001   35 
           149Sm spins.                                           14306001   36 
           (20240212A) OS. REACTION corrected in subs.2,4         14306001   37 
           (20250815A) BP: Updated STATUS, added the "effective   14306001   38 
                       cross sections" meaning "capture yield     14306001   39 
                       divided by the areal density".             14306001   40 
STATUS     (APRVD) Approved by P.E.Koehler on August 29, 2025.    14306001   41
